items.title: Israelis protest after their government’s Gaza City takeover decision | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Large crowds have gathered for this week's anti-government protests in Israel — the first since Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed plans for a new ground offensive aimed at taking complete control of Gaza’s largest city. 

The protests have drawn tens of thousands to the streets across the nation. Demonstrators fear the government's plans will further escalate the now 22-month war and endanger the lives of hostages.

DW Middle East correspondent Tania Krämer reports from Tel Aviv.

#israel #gaza #hostagerelease

items.title: What deal do Trump and Putin plan to strike over Ukraine’s future in Alaska on August 15? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump says he will meet Russian President Vladimir Putin in Alaska on August 15 to discuss a potential end to the war in Ukraine. It will be the first in-person encounter between the two leaders since Russia's invasion of Ukraine — and since Trump's return to office in January. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has warned that any deal excluding Kyiv would go nowhere. 

0:00 Russia’s President Putin has agreed to travel to Alaska to meet Donald Trump

2:49 Domitilla Sagramoso, Expert on Russian foreign and security policy at King's College London

6:18 Michael Kimmage, Director of the Wilson Center's Kennan Institute and former US state department expert on Russia and Ukraine

9:34 Ivana Stradner, Research fellow with the Foundation for Defense of Democracies, Washington D.C.

#trump #putin #ukraine

items.title: Japanese city Nagasaki marks 80 years since the atomic bombing | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>People in Nagasaki gathered to honor the victims of the 1945 bombing, which instantly killed 27,000 and led to tens of thousands more deaths from radiation. Three days earlier, Hiroshima was bombed, killing around 140,000.
 
At the city’s Peace Park, survivors, families, and representatives from 95 countries took part in a solemn ceremony — including a moment of silence at the exact time the bomb exploded.

#dwnewsshorts

items.title: Trump seals peace deal between longtime foes Armenia and Azerbaijan | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Azerbaijan and Armenia have signed a US-brokered peace deal aimed at ending nearly forty years of hostilities.
US President Donald Trump hosted Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev at the White House. The peace agreement reopens key transportation routes in the South Caucasus region as part of a long process to resolve the conflict.

00:00 Azerbaijan and Armenia sign US-brokered peace deal
03:01 DW speaks with South Caucasus analyst Olesya Vartanyan

items.title: Germany halts arms to Israel: What it means for Israel and Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany, one of Israel’s staunchest allies, has decided it no longer wants to provide weapons for the war in Gaza. The move comes after Israel's Security Cabinet approved Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's plan to reoccupy parts of the Gaza Strip. It's a significant announcement from Berlin. After the US, Germany is the second largest supplier of weapons to Israel. Netanyahu expressed his disappointment in a telephone call with Chancellor Friedrich Merz. The German leader has said he is deeply concerned about the continued suffering of Palestinians and that his government can't see how an expanded military offensive will help Israel achieve its objectives in the war. 

00:00 Germany suspends military exports to Israel
01:00 DW's Alexander Gerst reports from Berlin
06:10 DW speaks with Middle East expert and historian René Wildangel

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Chinese authorities attempt to limit spread of chikungunya virus | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>China and Hong Kong are currently experiencing an outbreak of the chikungunya virus, which is transmitted by mosquitoes. Thousands of cases have been recorded since June. The virus is also prevalent in Africa, Asia, and the Americas, and experts warn that it could spread to other regions due to climate change. Meanwhile authorities in southern China are trying to limit the outbreak. 

Chapters:
0:00 The virus spreading in China
1:00 Krutika Kuppalli, University of Texas
5:14 Carolina Geadas, Brigham and Women's Hospital

items.title: Which countries have nukes? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The world has marked the 80th anniversary of the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Meanwhile, an increasing number of countries are rediscovering that having nukes makes them powerful players on the global stage. There are still over 12,000 nuclear weapons in existence on the planet. According to The Federation of American Scientists, the exact number of nuclear weapons in the possession of each country is a closely held national secret.

items.title: Why the US lacks leverage over China on trade | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>With the US-China trade truce set to expire within days, Washington faces mounting pressure to secure a new deal. Former “phase one” commitments largely went unenforced, and Beijing now wields significant leverage through rare earth exports, technology access, and industrial overcapacity. President Trump is reportedly pushing for a quick agreement, even softening chip export controls, while China maintains strong positions in key supply chains. DW's Kate Ferguson speaks to economist George Magnus of Oxford University's China Center.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Trump confirms summit with Putin as Kremlin ignores Trump's deadline to make peace | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump says he's still ready to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin, despite Putin's refusal to include Ukrainian President Volodymr Zelenskyy in a proposed summit. Trump has been pressing for a meeting with Putin, as he seeks to secure an end to the war in Ukraine. He's given Putin until today to agree a ceasefire, or face severe economic punishment.
